steps: The graph models a projectile launched upward. Such a path forms a downward-opening parabola: height increases from launch, reaches a maximum (vertex), then decreases back toward the ground.
Reading the graph, the vertex (maximum height) occurs at \(x = 2\) seconds. Before \(x = 2\) the height is rising; after \(x = 2\) the height is falling.

An interval satisfies “increasing for the entire interval” only if every moment within it lies strictly before (or at) the vertex. The interval from \(x = 0\) to \(x = 2\) lies entirely in the rising portion, so the height is increasing throughout.

Intervals extending beyond \(x = 2\) (choices B and C reaching x = 3 or x = 4) include the descending portion and therefore fail the condition. The interval from \(x = 3\) to \(x = 4\) is entirely after the vertex, so the height is decreasing throughout.

choices.explanation: • The vertex is at \(x = 2\). From \(x = 0\) to \(x = 2\) the parabola is on its ascending branch, so height increases over the entire interval. That matches choice A — A is correct.
• This interval crosses the vertex at \(x = 2\). Height increases from 0 to 2, then decreases from 2 to 4. The height is not increasing for the entire interval. This is incorrect.
• The vertex is at \(x = 2\), so by \(x = 2\) the object has already reached its peak and begins descending. The height is decreasing over this interval, not increasing. This is incorrect.
• Both \(x = 3\) and \(x = 4\) are past the vertex at \(x = 2\), placing this interval entirely on the descending branch. The height is decreasing throughout. This is incorrect.
